subject: A new and increased Impact Factor for BMC Structural Biology
body:
BMC Structural Biology <http://www.biomedcentral.com/bmcstructbiol/> 

-------------------------------------------------------------------

Dear Colleague,

We are contacting you, as you are a BioMed Central registrant with an interest
in structural biology, to let you know that BMC Structural Biology
<http://www.biomedcentral.com/bmcstructbiol/> has just received its new Impact
Factor - an impressive 3.06. This is an increase on last year's Impact Factor
and the journal is now ranked 23rd of 69 in the field of Biophysics in the
Journal Citation Report.

We are sure you will agree that this Impact Factor will add significantly to
BMC Structural Biology's excellent reputation, and will help to further
establish it within the field. We also hope that you will consider the journal
when deciding where to submit
<http://www.biomedcentral.com/manuscript/bmcstructbiol> your next manuscript. 

The speed with which BMC Structural Biology has established its high standing
in the field demonstrates both the commitment of the editorial group
<http://www.biomedcentral.com/bmcstructbiol/edboard/> and the power of open
access. Free and unrestricted availability via the internet increases the
visibility of your research and thereby encourages its citation.

BMC Structural Biology <http://www.biomedcentral.com/bmcstructbiol> now boasts
a wide, international readership. Last month alone, articles in BMC Structural
Biology were accessed over 15,000 times from the journal's website
<http://www.biomedcentral.com/bmcstructbiol>, in addition to access via the
PubMed Central archive.

To submit <http://www.biomedcentral.com/manuscript/bmcstructbiol> your next
manuscript, or to sign up to receive regular email article alerts
<http://www.biomedcentral.com/bmcstructbiol/alerts>, please visit the journal
website.

With all best wishes,

Melissa Norton, MD 
Editor-in-Chief 
BMC series journals

Penelope Webb, PhD
Biology Editor
BMC series journals 

-------------------------------------------------------------------

You have received this message because you have asked for updates from 
BioMed Central and you have specified an interest in structural biology.
Your registered email address is: jeeproject@yahoo.com
To change your preferences please visit:
http://www.biomedcentral.com/my/details/interests 
To unsubscribe send a blank email to: leave-2440222-100446702.d998375336c8d9083c80b2a5090b461b@news.biomedcentral.com
For further information or enquiries please contact Customer Services at:
info@biomedcentral.com 

-------------------------------------------------------------------
	
BioMed Central Ltd, Science Navigation Group, Middlesex House, 
34-42 Cleveland Street, London, W1T 4LB, United Kingdom

Dear Colleague,

We are contacting you, as you are a BioMed Central registrant with an interest in structural biology, to let you know that BMC Structural Biology has just received its new Impact Factor - an impressive 3.06. This is an increase on last year's Impact Factor and the journal is now ranked 23rd of 69 in the field of Biophysics in the Journal Citation Report.

We are sure you will agree that this Impact Factor will add significantly to BMC Structural Biology's excellent reputation, and will help to further establish it within the field. We also hope that you will consider the journal when deciding where to submit your next manuscript.

The speed with which BMC Structural Biology has established its high standing in the field demonstrates both the commitment of the editorial group and the power of open access. Free and unrestricted availability via the internet increases the visibility of your research and thereby encourages its citation.

BMC Structural Biology now boasts a wide, international readership. Last month alone, articles in BMC Structural Biology were accessed over 15,000 times from the journal's website, in addition to access via the PubMed Central archive.
